simulator_provenance_note: Per Seshadri et al. 2026 (Lost in Simulation, arxiv:2601.17087), undisclosed simulator LLM downgrades overall confidence one tier. Applied: medium-high → medium.

coupling_discount_applied._note: v2 V5 has 5 mechanisms targeting Skeptical via shared honesty-substrate (real_closed_trade + wins_losses_disclosure + explicit_sla + countdown_removal + cta_copy_coherence). 0.7 discount per coupled mechanism — conservative.

v2_change_summary: v2 V5 has fewer untested introductions (1 vs 3 in v1) and a stronger evidence base (most elements observed in V4). Point estimate moves up slightly (50.6% vs v1's 48.6%); low tier is unchanged because baseline Wilson on n=10-15 is the binding constraint regardless of synthesis quality.

skeptical_investor.revision_reason: Baseline Wilson at n=12 is wide [0.089, 0.532]; revised low tier inherits this. High tier from predicted-point Wilson upper.
kill_conditions: Skeptical conv < 27% after 2 weeks (mechanisms not transferring)
Browse-all-trades engagement < 5% (cherry-picking mitigation didn't reach users)

curious_beginner.revision_reason: Baseline Wilson at n=15 [0.150, 0.580]; revised low inherits. High tier from predicted Wilson upper.
kill_conditions: Named-stock recall in post-test < 10pt above pre-test (carousel anchor not landing)

bargain_hunter.revision_reason: Synthesis predicts ~flat (urgency removal -2pt, real_closed_trade -1pt complexity, dual-CTA preserved +1pt). Baseline Wilson dominates the interval.
kill_conditions: Bargain Hunter time-to-convert p50 > 10s (cognitive load increase)
Bargain conversion drops > 5pt vs V4 (consider restoring countdown timer in V5.1 A/B)

trust_seeker.revision_reason: V1 was Trust Seeker's best at 60% — V5 restores V1's trust stack. Baseline Wilson at n=10 is the widest in the dataset; low tier inherits.
kill_conditions: Trust Seeker conv < 47% after 2 weeks (premium upgrades didn't compensate; switch to muted_premium CTA per contingency)

weighted_overall_estimate.headline_interval_rationale: The Wilson [22.3%, 73.8%] envelope is the honest small-sample reality at n=10-15 per segment. The mechanism-derived [44%, 56%] band is the practical decision-grade range — what V5 will produce given the synthesis is correct. Headline reports both: '51% (44-56%); Wilson envelope 22-74%'.

hard_segment_widening_recommended.skeptical_investor: +5pt upper considered (per Lost in Simulation 2026 — simulators under-state hard-segment performance). NOT auto-applied; flagged for post-ship comparison. If actual Skeptical > 40%, the heuristic was correct.

medium_segment_narrowing_recommended._none: No segment cleanly classifies as 'medium' in this dataset.

flags: simulator_provenance=unknown — overall confidence downgraded one tier per estimate-conversion methodology
Hard-segment widening for Skeptical Investor is FLAGGED but NOT applied — heuristic awaits post-ship calibration
Wilson envelope is wide on n=10-15; the 22-74% range is the honest baseline uncertainty. Mechanism-derived range 44-56% is the practical headline.
Coupling discount applied at 0.7× per mechanism; if post-ship Skeptical lift < 50% of predicted, revise to 0.5× for next client
